text.title: Tech Radar (ThoughtWorks-Style) Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules grounding the tech-radar-thoughtworks-style methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check with sourced rationale. The terminal `skip-this-methodology` rule is the safety valve invoked by the decision tree when preconditions fail.

rule.statement: Radar MUST use exactly four quadrants: Adopt / Trial / Assess / Hold — no custom.
Every entry MUST cite ≥1 evidence anchor (ADR, RFC, incident, benchmark).
Hold entries MUST name a replacement or migration path.

rule.rationale: Stale radars mislead teams within 6 months.

rule.statement: Each entry MUST name an owner accountable for that ring assignment.
Author MUST stop and route elsewhere when the decision-tree resolves to a skip leaf; partial application of this methodology against an unfit input is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Forcing the methodology onto a mismatched input produces low-quality artefacts and erodes trust in the corpus.
